Package i5.cb.telos.frame

Class TelosFrame

  • java.lang.Object
    • i5.ShallowCloneable
      • i5.cb.telos.frame.ShallowCloneableNode
        • i5.cb.telos.frame.TelosFrame
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    AST_Node, java.io.Serializable, java.lang.Cloneable

    public class TelosFrame
extends ShallowCloneableNode
implements AST_Node
    A single Telos frame. May be used to access frames received from the parser as well as to construct arbitrary frames. This is recommended rather than constructing Telos frames as strings because of better encapsulation of the Telos syntax.
    (mutable)
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors

      TelosFrame​(ObjectName _objectName)
      constructs a Telos frame without classes, superclasses or attributes

      constructs a Telos frame with the given Omega class, object name, classes, superclasses and attributes

    • Constructor Detail

      • TelosFrame

        public TelosFrame​(ObjectName _inOmegaSpec,
                  ObjectName _objectName,
                  ObjectNames _inSpec,
                  ObjectNames _isaSpec,
                  WithSpec _withSpec)
        constructs a Telos frame with the given Omega class, object name, classes, superclasses and attributes
        Parameters:
        _inOmegaSpec - the 'Omega class' 'this' is in (optional)
        _objectName - the name of this frame
        _inSpec - the classes 'this' is in (optional)
        _isaSpec - the superclasses 'this' is derived from (optional)
        _withSpec - definition of attributes (optional)

      • TelosFrame

        public TelosFrame​(ObjectName _objectName)
        constructs a Telos frame without classes, superclasses or attributes
        Parameters:
        _objectName - the name of this frame

    • Method Detail

      • inOmegaSpec

        public final ObjectName inOmegaSpec​()
        Returns:
        != null: the Omega class 'this' is in == null: there is no Omega class 'this' is in

      • setInOmegaSpec

        public final void setInOmegaSpec​(ObjectName _inOmegaSpec)
        sets the Omega classes 'this' is in

        public final void setInSpec​(ObjectNames _inSpec)
        sets the classes 'this' is in
        Parameters:
        _inSpec - != null: the classes 'this' is in == null: there are no classes 'this' is in (same as empty _inSpec)

      • isaSpec

        public final ObjectNames isaSpec​()
        Returns:
        the subclasses 'this' is derived from

      • setIsaSpec

        public final void setIsaSpec​(ObjectNames _isaSpec)
        sets the superclasses 'this' is derived from
        Parameters:
        _isaSpec - != null: the superclasses 'this' is derived from == null: there are no superclasses 'this' is derived from (same as empty list)

        Returns:

      • setWithSpec

        public final void setWithSpec​(WithSpec _withSpec)
        sets the superclasses 'this' is derived from
        Parameters:
        _withSpec - != null: definition of attributes (declarations) == null: same as empty _withSpec (no declarations)

        Returns:
        does 'this' have subclasses specified?

        public final boolean hasWithSpec​()

        does 'this' have attributes specified?

      • writeTelos

        public void writeTelos​(java.io.DataOutputStream os)
                throws java.io.IOException
        Description copied from interface: AST_Node
        writes the text representation of this object (node and subnodes) in 'Plain Aachen' syntax. Writing into an OutputStream is *much* more efficient than recursively constructing a String. To write into a String, do the following:

        ByteArrayOutputStream bos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
        DataOutputStream dos = new DataOutputStream( bos );
        frames.writeTelos( dos );
        String s = bos.toString();
        Specified by:
        writeTelos in interface AST_Node
        Parameters:
        os - the OutputStream to write into
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException
        See Also:
        AST_Node.writeTelos( DataOutputStream )

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString​()
        Overrides:
        toString in class ShallowCloneableNode
        Returns:
        Text (Telos) representation of this (uses writeTelos())

        public java.lang.String toSMLFragment​()

      • equals

        public boolean equals​(java.lang.Object other)
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        See Also:
        equals( TelosFrame )

      • equals

        public final boolean equals​(TelosFrame other)
        As a TelosFrame object represents a syntactic Telos object, equals compares two Telos frames on a syntactical basis. Example: The two frames a in A, B end and a in B, A end are not equal. The same applies to the equals function of all other AST_Node's.

      • inOmegaSpecIsEqual

        public final boolean inOmegaSpecIsEqual​(TelosFrame other)
        Returns:
        the inOmega spec of the two frames is syntactically equal.

      • inSpecIsEqual

        public final boolean inSpecIsEqual​(TelosFrame other)
        Returns:
        the in spec of the two frames is syntactically equal.

      • isaSpecIsEqual

        public final boolean isaSpecIsEqual​(TelosFrame other)
        Returns:
        the isa spec of the two frames is syntactically equal.

      • withSpecIsEqual

        public final boolean withSpecIsEqual​(TelosFrame other)
        Returns:
        the with spec of the two frames is syntactically equal.

      • getTargetOf

        public PropertyTarget getTargetOf​(Label source)
        Returns:
        the value (target) of the attribute (property) with the given source. == null, if there is no attribute with the given source. Example: In the frame

        Employee John with
        name
        johnsName : "John"
        end

        getTargetOf( new Label( "johnsName" ) ) would return "John".
        The Telos syntax allows a frame with two different properties that have the same source (but different targets). However, such a frame is semantically incorrect. For the sake of simplicity, we just return the first occurence of a property with the given source.

      • getPropertiesInCategory

        public com.objectspace.jgl.Set getPropertiesInCategory​(Label category)
        Returns:
        the set of properties in the given category

      • getCategories

        public com.objectspace.jgl.Set getCategories​()
        Returns:
        the set of all attribute categories in the frame

      • getCategoriesOf

        public com.objectspace.jgl.Set getCategoriesOf​(Label source)
        Returns:
        the set of categories which the property with the given source is in. Empty set, if there is no such property.

                         PropertyTarget attrTarget)

        attrTarget - the target of the attribute
